claim: PM1009 is an anti-TIGIT/anti-PVRIG bispecific antibody built from a human IgG1 anti-TIGIT monoclonal antibody with a PVRIG-targeting scFv fused to the C terminus.
evidence: NCI Drug Dictionary describes PM1009 as a human IgG1 anti-TIGIT monoclonal antibody with a PVRIG-targeting scFv fused to the C-terminus.

claim: PM1009 blocks both TIGIT and PVRIG pathways and is designed to shift CD112/CD155 signaling toward CD226/DNAM-1 costimulation.
evidence: NCI states that TIGIT inhibition prevents ligand interaction, enhances CD112/CD155 interaction with CD226, and PVRIG inhibition blocks PVRIG-CD112/PVRL2 inhibition of CTL/NK activation.

claim: ClinicalTrials describes PM1009 as a fully human anti-TIGIT x PVRIG bispecific with wildtype IgG1 Fc, high monovalent affinity to each target, and simultaneous TIGIT/PVRIG binding.
evidence: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05607563 brief summary and detailed description include these format and study design statements.

claim: SIM0348 is a humanized IgG1-based anti-TIGIT/anti-PVRIG bispecific antibody that simultaneously inhibits TIGIT and PVRIG.
evidence: NCI Drug Dictionary describes SIM0348 as a humanized IgG1-based bispecific antibody against TIGIT and PVRIG.

claim: SIM0348 adds an Fc-mediated Treg-depletion/tumor-killing mechanism on top of checkpoint blockade.
evidence: NCI and Simcere materials emphasize Fc-mediated killing of TIGIT-expressing Tregs and Tregs co-expressing TIGIT/PVRIG.
source: NCI Drug Dictionary SIM0348; Simcere announcement 2023-04-03

claim: Preclinical SIM0348 data support stronger immune activation than single TIGIT or PVRIG blockade and potential PD-L1-combination benefit.
evidence: Simcere announcement says preclinical studies showed NK-cell killing, IFN-gamma secretion by antigen-specific CD8+ T cells, higher efficacy than PVRIG or TIGIT monotherapy, and strong anti-PD-L1 combination efficacy in models.

claim: The 2025 first-in-human phase I abstract reports receptor occupancy, tolerability, and combination activity signals for SIM0348 plus sintilimab.
evidence: Annals of Oncology abstract reports NCT05718219, 49 participants by Apr 30 2025, MTD not reached, near-complete TIGIT/PVRIG receptor occupancy at selected doses, and ORR signals in combination therapy including advanced NSCLC.
